content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. The difference is that you remember all array elements correctly, and there is no Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0. It is worth 40 points in Division 2, 3 and 4. It is non-scoreable in Division 1.**

You have an array AAA of NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.

You can perform the following operation as many times as you want:


Choose L,RL, RL,R such that 1≤L<R≤N1 \le L < R \le N1≤L<R≤N and AL=ARA_L = A_RAL​=AR​, then update Ai:=ALA_i := A_LAi​:=AL​ for all L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R.
Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the minimum number of distinct elements in the array AAA after applying operations optimally.

In this version of the problem, you remember all array elements correctly, and you just need to answer the value of f(A)f(A)f(A) for the given array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of distinct elements.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 and the array becomes [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1] with only 111 distinct element.
Test case 222: Do no operation. The array has 222 distinct elements. It is impossible to make it lower.
